text:
Admission Date:  2014-04-02              Discharge Date:   1994-10-18

Date of Birth:  1980-02-04             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Sulfamethoxazole / Motrin / Depakote / Reglan

Attending:Jerry
Chief Complaint:
Altered MS

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
55 y/o F with PMHx significant for neurosarcoidosis,
panhypopituitarism, DM, HTN, presented on 12-13 w/ 2 to 3 days
of malaise and mental status changes. Hx obtained through friend
as pt somnolent, lethargic, w/ sluggish speech. Pt slept for
approx 2 days missing all meals and meds.
*
No fevers, chills, no cough, HA, no abd pain, patient initially
refused LP. Had head CT, unchanged from previous, MRI done 03-12
unchanged from previous per neuro read. Negative UA, CXR, but pt
given empiric Vanco/Ceftriaxone bc of suspicion for sepsis. No
source identified and NO BLOOD CTX SENT.
*
Pt given hydrocortisone 100 mg IV one morning on the floor, but
somehow missed other doses during day, despite being written.
During the day, the pt's BP drifted downwards.  Was transferred
to the MICU service for management of hypotension.  Mental
status and blood pressure improved with boluses of NS and
stress-dose steroids.
*
On transfer to the floor, the patient reports doing well.  She
remembers missing her medications prior to admission, but does
not remember anything else.  She currently denies CP/SOB,
dizziness, HA, or abdominal pain.  She does report upper back
pain, which she reports is unchanged from her baseline.

Past Medical History:
1. Neurosarcoidosis
2. Panhypopituitarism.
3. Status post right temporal craniotomy for brain biopsy.
4. Diabetes insipidus.
5. Diabetes mellitus type 2.
6. Questionable gastroparesis in the past.
7. Hypertension.
8. Hypercholesterolemia.
9. Migraines.
10. Gastroesophageal reflux disease.
11. History of upper gastrointestinal bleed.
12. Anemia.
13. Obesity.
14. History of subarachnoid hemorrhage 20 years ago.
15. Shingles.
16. L4 through L5 disc disease.
17. Stroke with left hemiparesis in 2013


Social History:
The patient denies any tobacco. No alcohol or intravenous drug
abuse. She lives with a friend. She is originally from [**Country **]
and has family support locally. She currently does not work.
She lives with a friend in Martin Montoya.

Family History:
noncontributory

Physical Exam:

clear

ABD: Soft, NT, ND, +NABS. No rebound or guarding. No HSM.
EXT: Warm and well-perfused. No C/C/E.
SKIN: No rashes.
NEURO: A&Ox2

*
CXR (1980-10-19):  No acute pulmonary process
*
HEAD CT (1980-10-19): The exam is unchanged from the prior. The
ventricles are prominent, but are unchanged in size. There are
scattered low attenuation white matter changes which are stable.
The appearance of the infundibulum and hypothalamus, which is
the site of the known mass, does not appear significantly
changed in the interval. There is no intra- or extraaxial
hemorrhage. The Holder/white matter differentiation is preserved.
The cisterns are unremarkable.  The osseous structures are
unremarkable. The patient is status-post right frontal
craniotomy. The mastoid air cells are normally aerated. There is
focal mucosal thickening in the left maxillary sinus.
IMPRESSION
1. Unchanged appearance of the head from 2002-11-08.
2. Stable appearance of the infundibulum and hypothalamus. An
MRI with
gadolinium contrast is recommended to evaluate for subtle
changes in this
region.
3. Maxillary sinus mucosal disease as described above.
*
MR HEAD W & W/O CONTRAST  2014-04-02 10:28 AM
1. No evidence of acute ischemia.
2. Interval enlargement in the area of enhancement in the
hypothalamus.
3. Increased extent of bilateral white matter changes which are
of uncertain etiology but may be secondary to radiation therapy.
4. Stable appearance of multiple foci of increased
susceptibility.
5. Limited MRA of the circle of PSC 2747, Box 5678
APO AE 54715 with no gross
abnormalities.
*
EEG Study Date of 2014-04-02
ABNORMALITY #1: A slow and disorganized 03-23 Hz theta frequency
background was seen.
ABNORMALITY #2: Bursts of generalized 12-29 Hz delta frequency
slowing were seen.
BACKGROUND: As above.
HYPERVENTILATION: Was contraindicated due to the patient's
clinical state.
INTERMITTENT PHOTIC STIMULATION: Could not be obtained as the
test was requested to be done portably.
SLEEP: There were no normal sleep or wake transitions seen
though at times, some faster frequencies were seen in the
background to suggest arousal.
CARDIAC MONITOR: Demonstrated a regular but fast cardiac rhythm
at approximately 108 bpm.
IMPRESSION: This is an abnormal EEG due to the presence of a
slow and disorganized background with bursts of generalized
slowing consistent with a mild to moderate encephalopathy of
toxic, metabolic, or anoxic etiology. Note is incidentally made
of a sinus tachycardia on the cardiac monitor.
*
1984-06-10 12:21 pm URINE: PSEUDOMONAS AERUGINOSA
SENSITIVITIES: MIC expressed in MCG/ML

CEFEPIME--------------     2 S
CEFTAZIDIME-----------     4 S
CIPROFLOXACIN---------<=0.25 S
GENTAMICIN------------   <=1 S
IMIPENEM--------------     2 S
MEROPENEM-------------     1 S
PIPERACILLIN----------   <=4 S
PIPERACILLIN/TAZO-----     8 S
TOBRAMYCIN------------   <=1 S


Brief Hospital Course:
Ms. Todd is a 55 year-old female with a past medical history
significant for neurosarcoidosis, pan-hypopituitarism, diabetes,
and HTN, who presented on 1980-10-19 with 2 to 3 days of malaise
and mental status changes. She was initially admitted to
neurology service.  The intitial history was obtained through
friend because the patient was somnolent and lethargic, with
sluggish speech on admission.  The patient had slept for
approximately 2 days missing all of her meals and medications.
*
1) HYPOTENSION: Shortly after admission, while on the Neurology
Service, the patient became hypotensive.  She was transferred to
MICU where she received stress dose steroids for presumed
adrenal insufficiency given the patient had missed several doses
of her steroids.  She also received IV fluid rehydration.  After
stress dose steroids and fluid boluses her blood pressure
returned to 423 Austin Square
South Christinafurt, MP 86412 and her mental status improved.  At that
time, she was transferred out of the MICU to the Medicine
Service.  At the time of transfer, she was A&O x 3 and she was
normotensive.  After transfer to the floor, the hydrocortisone
was discontinued and she was put on a steroid taper.  This taper
will continue for a week until she reaches a daily dose of
Prednisone 10 mg PO QD.  On discharge, she was restarted on her
home bp meds.
*
2) PSEUDOMONAS UTI: The patient was found to have a low grade
fever on 1984-06-10.  Her urine culture was positive for a
pseudomonas UTI.  She also became hypotensive again and was
transferred the MICU again for stabilization.  There was also
concern that she had a seizure as well.  In the MICU, she was
given stress dose steroids again with Hydrocortisone 100 mg IV
Q8H.  She was also double covered with Cefepime and
Ciprofloxacin for her UTI.  Cultures have shown that the
pseudomonas is sensitive to both cefepime and ciprofloxacin.
After stabilization of her mental status and blood pressure, the
patient was transferred back to the medicine service.  At the
time of discharge, her cefepime was discontinued, but the
Ciprofloxacin was extended to complete a total 10 day course.
*
3) ALTERED MS: When admitted to the Neurology Service, she was
not fully alert and oriented x3.  She was also somnolent, but
arousable.  The cause of her altered mental status was thought
to be secondary to missing her medications for 2-3 days prior to
admission.   After receiving stress dose steroids
(Hydrocortisone 100 mg IV Q8H), her mental status improved
significantly.  By the time of discharge, she was A&O x 3.
*
4) NEUROSARCOIDOSIS: The patient is on Cytoxan with stable CT
and MRI imaging of the head.  There is no evidence clinical of
worsening disease.
*
5) TYPE 2 DIABETES: The patient is on Metformin 850 mg PO TID
and NPH 22 units QAM as an outpatient.  These were stopped
during this admission, and restarted at the time of discharge.
Her NPH was decreased to 10 units at the time of discharge.
While in the hospital, she was mainained on a regular insulin
sliding scale.
*
6) PAN-HYPOPITUITARISM: She was restarted on Synthryoid at 125
mcg PO daily.  She was also mainatined on her dose of DDAVP 10
mcg daily.  Her steroids were managed as outlined above in
sections 1 and 2.
*
7) H/O SEIZURES:  She was continued on her outpatient dose of
phenytoin 100 mg PO TID and gabapentin 300 mg PO TID.
*
8) HYPERCHOLESTEROLEMIA: She was continued on her outpatient
dose of lipitor.
*
9) PROPHYLAXIS:  She was continued on a bowel regimen, protonix,
and was given SC Heparin 5000 units TID.
*
10) CODE STATUS: Ms. Todd was full code during this
admission.
*
11) DISPOSITION: She was discharged to home with pt services.

Medications on Admission:
Metformin 850 mg PO BID
Prednisone 10 mg PO QD
DDAVP 0.01% 1/2 spray Kentfield Rehabilitation Hospital
Cytoxan
Neurontin 300 mg PO TID
Phenytoin
Lipitor 10 mg PO QD
Lisinopril 5 mg PO QD
Levoxyl 125 mcg PO QD
